title: NOC 22302 – Industrial Engineering and Manufacturing Technologists and Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Industrial Engineering and Manufacturing Technologists and Technicians are in strong demand across Canada, driven by advanced manufacturing, automation adoption, supply-chain optimization, clean-technology facilities, and productivity upgrades across factories nationwide. Classified under NOC 22302 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Program robotics & CNC equipment using CAD/CAM
Assist with layout planning & production studies
employment_requirements: Two- to three-year diploma in industrial or manufacturing-engineering technology (technologists)
One- to two-year diploma for technicians
Around two years of supervised experience for certification
Trade certification & machining experience for CAD/CAM roles
In Quebec, regulatory-body membership to use the title Professional Technologist
job_outlook: This balanced outlook still favours candidates with:
pathways: Advancement into operations management, lean leadership, or automation roles
additional_information: Canadian employers are actively recruiting industrial-engineering professionals in:
Advanced-manufacturing plantsPulp & paper operationsPlastics & textile factoriesRobotics & CNC facilitiesQuality-assurance departmentsGovernment inspection unitsInsurance & risk-management firms
Annual salaries commonly range between $36,600–$86,600 CAD, depending on province, automation skills, and production-sector specialization.
